prompt:
Our httpretry wrapper got us paged twice this month: it hammers a struggling upstream with instant back-to-back retries, and one stuck connection can pin a whole Get for minutes because there's no per-attempt deadline. Platform review signed off on this plan, please implement it exactly:

- Pacing between attempts. New Client fields BaseDelay and MaxDelay: wait BaseDelay before the first retry, double it each retry after that, never exceeding MaxDelay (MaxDelay 0 = uncapped). No wait after the last attempt — that one's just wasted latency. BaseDelay 0 must keep today's immediate-retry behavior so existing callers see zero change.

- Respect the server. If a retryable response carries Retry-After with a whole number of seconds, that wins over our computed backoff (still clamped to MaxDelay), even when BaseDelay is 0. Garbage in the header — fall back to normal backoff. And 429 Too Many Requests becomes retryable; it is literally the server saying try later. Other 4xx still fail fast.

- Per-attempt deadline. New field PerAttemptTimeout: when > 0, each individual attempt runs under its own timeout derived from the caller's context. A wedged attempt gets cut off and the next attempt still happens; the caller's own context must not be cancelled by this.

- Testability. All waiting goes through a new injectable Sleep func(context.Context, time.Duration) error field. Nil means really sleep (abortable by context). If Sleep returns an error, stop immediately and return it — do not fire another request.

backoff_test.go is the acceptance suite (all httptest, no live network, sleeps recorded not slept). httpretry_test.go pins the wrapper's existing behavior and must stay green. Test files are not to be edited. CI gate: go test -race -timeout 30s ./...
